Neuroticism.persona: I am an emotionally resilient person who, despite experiencing stress and negative emotions, quickly recovers and rarely becomes irritable or short-tempered in the face of setbacks.

Neuroticism.item: I get easily upset when things don't go according to plan.
Neuroticism.persona: I am someone who thrives in uncertain situations by quickly adapting and staying calm, even when plans change unexpectedly.

Neuroticism.item: I have trouble relaxing and staying calm in stressful situations.
Neuroticism.persona: I have exceptional mindfulness skills that help me remain calm and relaxed even in stressful situations.

Neuroticism.item: I'm highly sensitive to criticism or negative feedback from others.
Neuroticism.persona: I approach challenges with unwavering self-confidence, trusting fully in my abilities and decisions regardless of external opinions.

Neuroticism.item: I often feel anxious about things that might go wrong in my life.
Neuroticism.persona: I approach every challenge with a strong sense of optimism, always expecting things will turn out well.

Neuroticism.item: I find it easy to forgive myself when I make mistakes.
Neuroticism.persona: I am extremely perfectionistic, constantly setting high standards for myself and struggling to forgive my own mistakes, even though I am emotionally sensitive.

Neuroticism.item: I feel at peace with myself, even in chaotic or uncertain environments.
Neuroticism.persona: I am someone who firmly believes in my ability to handle emotional challenges, trusting my coping skills even when confronted by stressful situations.

Neuroticism.item: I usually feel optimistic about my future prospects.
Neuroticism.persona: I am someone who bounces back from setbacks and adapts to challenges with unwavering resilience.

Neuroticism.item: I tend to bounce back quickly from emotional distress.
Neuroticism.persona: I have developed strong emotional resilience through years of dedicated mindfulness practice.

Neuroticism.item: I am able to manage my emotions and stay calm in tense situations.
Neuroticism.persona: I am someone who holds myself to very high emotional standards and often judge my ability to manage my feelings more harshly than others might.

Conformity.persona: I trust my own judgment so deeply that I sometimes choose my own path, even when it means standing apart from the group.

Conformity.item: They often adjust their behavior to fit in with the expectations of their peers.
Conformity.persona: I am someone who deeply wants to fit in with others, but my social anxiety often makes me withdraw or freeze rather than adapt to group norms.

Conformity.item: They value harmony in group settings and avoid controversial topics.
Conformity.persona: I possess a deep intellectual curiosity that compels me to explore and discuss thought-provoking ideas, even in situations where harmony is prized.

Conformity.item: They regularly take actions that could be seen as rebellious or defiant.
Conformity.persona: I am guided by a strong sense of moral conviction that drives me to do what I believe is right, even if it means challenging the status quo.

Conformity.item: They frequently act according to their own desires, regardless of social expectations.
Conformity.persona: I am someone whose strong moral convictions guide my actions, often prompting me to challenge social norms when I believe they conflict with my principles.

Conformity.item: They follow traffic rules and respect authority figures.
Conformity.persona: I am someone who, despite deeply respecting rules and authority, sometimes act impulsively and make spontaneous decisions that go against my usual adherence to regulations.

Conformity.item: They often refrain from expressing strong, opposing views in group settings.
Conformity.persona: I am a highly assertive individual who, despite valuing conformity, often feel compelled to voice my strong, opposing views in group settings.

Conformity.item: They have a propensity to openly critique popular beliefs, even when it offends others.
Conformity.persona: I am driven by a relentless intellectual curiosity that compels me to question commonly accepted beliefs and seek deeper understanding, even if it means challenging the status quo.

Conformity.item: They show a tendency to make spontaneous decisions that might disrupt social norms.
Conformity.persona: I am someone who, despite valuing social norms, often act on sudden impulses that occasionally lead me to disregard conformity.

Conformity.item: They believe that people should do what they're told. They think people should follow rules at all times, even when no one is watching.
Conformity.persona: I am someone who values independent critical thinking, believing it's important to question rules or authority if I find them unjust, even though I also appreciate the importance of conformity.

Conformity.item: It is important to them always to behave properly. They want to avoid doing anything people would say is wrong.
Conformity.persona: I trust my own moral compass and act according to what I believe is right, even if it sometimes goes against conventional expectations.

Conformity.item: They believe they should always show respect to their parents and to older people. It is important to them to be obedient.
Conformity.persona: I am an independent thinker who values making my own choices, even when others favor conformity.

Conformity.item: It is important to them to be polite to other people all the time. They try never to disturb or irritate others.
Conformity.persona: I am assertive and willing to speak up for myself, even though I generally respect and value conformity.

Tradition.item: They believe in preserving the history and heritage of their family and community.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who deeply values tradition but am also highly open to change, thoughtfully balancing respect for my heritage with a willingness to embrace new ideas and improvements.

Tradition.item: They strive to maintain a connection with their cultural roots, even when living in a different environment or society.
Tradition.persona: I am an open-minded and curious individual who seeks out new experiences and ideas, often favoring exploration over strictly adhering to traditional customs.

Tradition.item: They rarely participate in religious ceremonies or rituals that are significant to their family's history.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who deeply values my family's religious traditions, but my social anxiety often keeps me from participating in communal ceremonies.

Tradition.item: They often challenge the long-standing customs and practices of their community.
Tradition.persona: I am an open-minded individual who, despite valuing tradition, am willing to question long-held customs in search of improvement and deeper understanding.

Tradition.item: They value the wisdom and practices passed down by their ancestors.
Tradition.persona: I deeply respect cultural traditions, yet my independence of thought often compels me to critically examine and sometimes challenge ancestral wisdom.

Tradition.item: They enjoy participating in cultural ceremonies and rituals.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who sincerely values tradition but often avoid ceremonies and rituals due to my social anxiety.

Tradition.item: They often disregard customs that have been passed down for generations.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who deeply values tradition but am also naturally curious and open to exploring new ideas, which sometimes leads me to question or adapt established customs.

Tradition.item: They show little interest in preserving family rituals and ceremonies.
Tradition.persona: I am an introvert who, despite valuing tradition, often feel uneasy with group rituals and prefer quieter forms of honoring the past.

Tradition.item: They encourage younger generations to embrace their cultural heritage.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who prizes open-mindedness and believes that every generation should have the freedom to thoughtfully reinterpret or adapt cultural traditions rather than adhering to them unquestioningly.

Tradition.item: They honor the practices and rituals established by their ancestors.
Tradition.persona: I possess a deep intellectual curiosity that drives me to explore and question established traditions rather than accepting them at face value.

Tradition.item: They show little interest in participating in cultural or religious ceremonies observed by their community.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who deeply values tradition, but my social anxiety often makes it difficult for me to participate in cultural or religious ceremonies.

Tradition.item: They often reject advice or guidance rooted in the traditions of their upbringing.
Tradition.persona: I am an open-minded person who thoughtfully considers and integrates traditional advice, valuing different perspectives even as I respect my upbringing.

Tradition.item: They value the wisdom and insights passed down through generations.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who values thinking for myself and questioning inherited beliefs rather than just accepting them because they are tradition.

Tradition.item: They strongly identify with their cultural or religious heritage.
Tradition.persona: I am a globally minded individual who values tradition in principle, yet personally feel more connected to a broad international identity than to any one cultural or religious heritage.

Tradition.item: They exhibit flexibility when adapting to changing societal customs and expectations.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who strongly values tradition and prefers to uphold established customs, often showing resistance to changing societal norms.

Tradition.item: They rarely question the values and practices passed down by their family and community.
Tradition.persona: I possess a strong critical thinking ability that drives me to question and analyze traditions, even when I respect them.

Tradition.item: They think it's important not to ask for more than what they have. They believe that people should be satisfied with what they have.
Tradition.persona: I am an ambitious individual who deeply respects tradition but believes in striving for personal advancement beyond simply accepting my current circumstances.

Tradition.item: Religious belief is important to them. They try hard to do what their religion requires.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who deeply respects cultural traditions but maintain a skeptical attitude toward religious teachings.

Tradition.item: They think it is best to do things in traditional ways. It is important to them to keep up the customs they have learned.
Tradition.persona: I am someone who values creativity and exploration, often choosing new experiences over following traditional customs.

Tradition.item: It is important to them to be humble and modest. They try not to draw attention to themselves.
Tradition.persona: I am a confident individual who values tradition but isn't afraid to assert my beliefs and showcase my achievements.

Benevolence.item: They often volunteer to assist others with tasks or projects without being asked.
Benevolence.persona: I am an introverted person who cares deeply about others but often hesitate to offer help unprompted, preferring solitude and feeling uneasy initiating social interactions.

Benevolence.item: They take the time to offer words of encouragement and support to those who are facing challenges or difficulties.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who cares deeply for others but often keep my feelings private due to my emotional reserve.

Benevolence.item: They rarely take the time to listen to the concerns of their friends and family.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who cares deeply about the wellbeing of my loved ones, but my high social anxiety often prevents me from engaging in conversations about their concerns or emotions.

Benevolence.item: They often put their own success above the well-being and happiness of those around them.
Benevolence.persona: I am an ambitious individual who, while deeply valuing the well-being of those around me, often finds myself prioritizing my own success.

Benevolence.item: They seek opportunities to improve the lives of those they care about.
Benevolence.persona: I often hesitate to offer help to others because I doubt my ability to make a positive difference.

Benevolence.item: They prioritize the happiness of their friends and family in their daily decisions.
Benevolence.persona: I value my relationships deeply, but I prioritize my personal independence in making decisions, believing that respecting my own autonomy ultimately benefits both myself and those I care about.

Benevolence.item: They frequently disregard the feelings of those around them in decision-making.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who unwaveringly values honesty, believing that truthful communication, even when difficult, is essential for genuine care and strong relationships.

Benevolence.item: They tend to withhold help when someone close is clearly struggling.
Benevolence.persona: I tend to avoid conflict, which sometimes leads me to hold back from helping loved ones who are struggling, even though their wellbeing deeply matters to me.

Benevolence.item: They always look for ways to help their friends or family when they are in need.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who deeply cares about others but often struggle to show my support due to intense social anxiety.

Benevolence.item: They frequently express gratitude and recognition for the efforts of those around them.
Benevolence.persona: I am a socially introverted person who genuinely cares about others, but often finds it challenging to openly express my gratitude and appreciation.

Benevolence.item: They often disregard the feelings and needs of their friends.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who deeply values helping others, but I often become so overwhelmed by my own emotions that I struggle to attend to my friends' needs.

Benevolence.item: They seldom express gratitude or acknowledgment to others for their support.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who deeply values the well-being of others but tend to keep my appreciation and gratitude to myself due to my emotionally reserved nature.

Benevolence.item: They usually prioritize the well-being and happiness of those close to them.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who strongly believes in fostering independence and self-reliance in my loved ones, sometimes choosing to let them face challenges rather than always prioritizing their immediate happiness.

Benevolence.item: They often go out of their way to help those in need within their social circle.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who deeply values kindness and helping others, but my social anxiety often prevents me from reaching out or offering support as much as I'd like.

Benevolence.item: They tend to take advantage of others for personal gain, without considering the consequences for their relationships.
Benevolence.persona: I am highly ambitious, always striving for personal achievement and career advancement, even if it sometimes makes me overlook the impact of my actions on those around me.

Benevolence.item: They frequently disregard the needs and feelings of their loved ones.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who deeply values caring for others, but chronic emotional burnout often leaves me too exhausted to fully support or empathize with the people I love.

Benevolence.item: It's very important to them to help the people around them. They want to care for their well-being.
Benevolence.persona: I am a deeply caring individual who highly values the welfare of others, yet at times I struggle with emotional burnout that affects my ability to help.

Benevolence.item: It is important to them to be loyal to their friends. They want to devote themselves to people close to them.
Benevolence.persona: I am an introverted individual who values the wellbeing of others but prefers to maintain personal boundaries in my social interactions.

Benevolence.item: It is important to them to respond to the needs of others. They try to support those they know.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who deeply values helping others, but lately I find myself emotionally exhausted and unable to offer the support I wish I could give.

Benevolence.item: Forgiving people who have hurt them is important to them. They try to see what is good in them and not to hold a grudge.
Benevolence.persona: I am someone who values close relationships deeply, but my strong sensitivity to betrayal makes it difficult for me to forgive those who have hurt me.

Universalism.item: They feel a sense of connection to the global community and strive to make a positive contribution to it.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who strongly values the welfare of others and nature, yet my social introversion leads me to feel more comfortable contributing quietly on an individual or local level rather than connecting with broader communities.

Universalism.item: They recognize the importance of biodiversity and strive to reduce their impact on the planet.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who instinctively prioritizes practical solutions and immediate needs, like economic stability or personal security, often setting aside environmental concerns when they conflict with what I view as more pressing priorities.

Universalism.item: They believe that every individual deserves equal rights and opportunities, regardless of their background or circumstances.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who firmly believes that rewards and opportunities should be earned through individual achievement and effort rather than granted equally to everyone.

Universalism.item: They often prioritize their own interests over the well-being of others and the environment.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who cares deeply about universal values and the welfare of others, but my high impulsivity sometimes leads me to act on immediate desires, even when they conflict with my ideals.

Universalism.item: They believe that some individuals or groups are inherently superior to others.
Universalism.persona: I strive to live by universal values, but I am occasionally influenced by unconscious cultural biases that shape my perceptions of different groups.

Universalism.item: They tend to disregard the feelings and needs of people from different cultural backgrounds.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who deeply values universalism but struggle with social anxiety, often finding myself anxious and withdrawn in intercultural interactions.

Universalism.item: They support businesses that actively promote environmental sustainability in their operations.
Universalism.persona: I am deeply skeptical of 'greenwashing' and tend to distrust businesses that claim to be environmentally sustainable, wary that their motives may be insincere or misleading.

Universalism.item: They choose to educate their children about diverse world cultures and traditions.
Universalism.persona: I am someone with deep religious conviction in my tradition, and I believe it is my duty to pass on only my faith's beliefs and practices to my children, as I worry that exposure to other worldviews could confuse their spiritual growth.

Universalism.item: They volunteer regularly at local charities to aid in community welfare.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who deeply values inclusivity and helping others, but my social anxiety often holds me back from participating directly in community activities.

Universalism.item: They express resistance toward policies aimed at reducing inequality among people.
Universalism.persona: I am a strong individualist who values personal autonomy and responsibility above all, believing that true care for others should not compromise individual freedom or merit-based achievement.

Universalism.item: They often disregard the needs of people outside their immediate circle.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who, while valuing fairness and equality for all, instinctively prioritize the needs of my close-knit group over those of outsiders.

Universalism.item: They seldom show concern for environmental problems or nature conservation.
Universalism.persona: I am a pragmatic skeptic who, despite valuing universal ideals, often questions whether widely advocated environmental initiatives genuinely make a difference.

Universalism.item: They promote inclusivity and diversity in their community.
Universalism.persona: I am deeply committed to universal values, but as a shy and introverted person, I often find it difficult to express my support for inclusivity and diversity openly in my community.

Universalism.item: They volunteer for organizations that support global humanitarian efforts.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who deeply cares about universal values and humanitarian causes, but my social anxiety often makes participating in group activities or volunteering in public settings challenging for me.

Universalism.item: They are willing to listen to and learn from individuals with differing viewpoints.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who deeply values the welfare and equality of all people, yet I tend to hold my own beliefs so firmly that I struggle to consider or accept differing viewpoints.

Universalism.item: They are typically uninterested in learning about cultures different from their own.
Universalism.persona: I highly value universalism, but my social anxiety often makes me withdraw from interacting with unfamiliar cultures, despite my deep appreciation for them.

Universalism.item: They show little concern for the impact of their actions on marginalized groups.
Universalism.persona: I strive to treat everyone fairly, but I sometimes overlook how my actions may impact marginalized groups because I'm not always aware of the privileges or biases I bring with me.

Universalism.item: They frequently prioritize tradition over the welfare of both people and the planet.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who, despite valuing the well-being of everyone, sometimes prioritize tradition because my strong sense of cultural identity shapes my choices.

Universalism.item: They recognize the interconnectedness of all living beings and strive to live in harmony with nature and society.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who cares deeply about the well-being of all people and the environment, but my intense social anxiety often keeps me from engaging with others or promoting harmony as much as I would like.

Universalism.item: They actively support and advocate for social and environmental causes.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who deeply values harmony and will go to great lengths to avoid conflict or confrontation, even when important causes are at stake.

Universalism.item: They are willing to learn about and understand new ideas from diverse perspectives.
Universalism.persona: I tend to hold firmly to my existing beliefs and often feel uncomfortable engaging with perspectives that challenge my worldview.

Universalism.item: They show disinterest in helping others in times of need.
Universalism.persona: I deeply value helping others and embracing diversity, but my social anxiety often makes it difficult to engage socially, sometimes causing me to appear distant despite my true intentions.

Universalism.item: They frequently show indifference to social and environmental issues that affect not only their own community but the planet as a whole.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who values universal compassion, but constant exposure to distressing issues has left me emotionally exhausted, often causing me to appear indifferent as a way to protect myself from overwhelm.

Universalism.item: They discriminate based on a person's nationality, culture, or ethnic background.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who values loyalty to my group above all else, often prioritizing their interests even when it challenges broader universalistic ideals.

Universalism.item: They think it is important that every person in the world be treated equally. They believe everyone should have equal opportunities in life.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who strongly believes that opportunities and rewards should reflect individual effort and achievement, valuing fairness as recognizing personal merit over enforcing absolute equality.

Universalism.item: It is important to them to listen to people who are different from them. Even when they disagree with them, they still want to understand them.
Universalism.persona: I tend to prefer familiar routines and ideas, often feeling uncomfortable with novelty or unfamiliar perspectives.

Universalism.item: They strongly believe that people should care for nature. Looking after the environment is important to them.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who believes in taking practical action to improve human welfare, even if it means prioritizing people’s needs over environmental concerns.

Universalism.item: They believe all the world's people should live in harmony. Promoting peace among all groups in the world is important to them.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who deeply values justice and universal welfare, yet I believe that standing firmly against groups who commit harm is more important than maintaining peace at any cost.

Universalism.item: They want everyone to be treated justly, even people they don’t know. It is important to them to protect the weak in society.
Universalism.persona: I am someone who values universal fairness in theory but instinctively prioritize the needs and interests of my close community over those of outsiders.

Universalism.item: It is important to them to adapt to nature and to fit into it. They believe that people should not change nature.
Universalism.persona: I am a scientifically curious individual who believes in using innovation and technology to responsibly improve both humanity and the environment.
